leadText: Headbutt (Japanese: ずつき Headbutt) is a damage-dealing Normal-type move introduced in Generation I. It is: TM02 in Generation II. TM01 in Pokémon: Let's Go, Pikachu! and Let's Go, Eevee! and Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 In battle 1.1.1 Generation IX 1.1.1.1 Pokémon Legends: Z-A 1.2 Outside of battle 1.2.1 Generation II 1.2.2 Generation III 1.2.3 Generation IV 1.2.4 Generation V onwards Effect [ edit | edit source ] In battle [ edit | edit source ] Headbutt does damage and has a 30% chance of causing the target to flinch . Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Z-A [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Z-A Category Physical Accuracy 100% Standard Plus Move Power 70 84 Cooldown 7s Move wind-up time 15 frames Effect duration —s Additional effect — — — None Outside of battle [ edit | edit source ] Main article: Headbutt tree Generation II [ edit | edit source ] Headbutt became a move which can be used outside of battle, allowing the player to shake small trees, sometimes causing a wild Pokémon to drop out. Depending on the time of day, the Pokémon which falls out may be asleep . Some Pokémon can only be caught by this method (e.g., Pineco , Heracross ). Generation III [ edit | edit source ] Headbutt is no longer a field move. Headbutt can be used as the second move of a Pokémon Contest combination , with the user gaining 2 bonus appeal points if Focus Energy was used in the previous turn. Generation IV [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Diamond, Pearl , and Platinum , Headbutt is not a field move. In Pokémon HeartGold and SoulSilver , Headbutt can again be used on trees in the field. However, now it can be used on all trees, but only some will yield Pokémon; trees that can yield Pokémon will always yield Pokémon when headbutted. Also, Pokémon will not be asleep when they are encountered this way, regardless of the time of day. Furthermore, categories of trees have been abandoned, in favor of unique wild Pokémon for each location. There are also now special trees, which in each area yield a Pokémon not normally encountered by using Headbutt in that area. After the National Pokédex is acquired, non-regional Pokémon from Hoenn or Sinnoh can also be found using this method. Generation V onwards [ edit | edit source ] Headbutt is no longer a field move.

sections.title: In battle
sections.text: In battle [ edit | edit source ] Headbutt does damage and has a 30% chance of causing the target to flinch . Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Z-A [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Z-A Category Physical Accuracy 100% Standard Plus Move Power 70 84 Cooldown 7s Move wind-up time 15 frames Effect duration —s Additional effect — — — None

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description Stad A Normal -type attack. Has a one-in-three chance of making the target flinch if it connects. Stad2 A Normal -type attack. Has a one-in-three chance of making the target flinch if it hits. G S C An attack that may make foe flinch. R S E Colo XD A ramming attack that may cause flinching. FR LG The user sticks its head out and rams. It may make the foe flinch. D P Pt PBR The user sticks its head out and rams straight forward. It may make the foe flinch. HG SS The user attacks with its head. It may make the foe flinch. To find Pokémon, hit trees. B W B2 W2 The user sticks out its head and attacks by charging straight into the target. It may also make the target flinch. X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA S V The user sticks out its head and attacks by charging straight into the target. This may also make the target flinch. ZA The user attacks by leaning forward and charging headfirst into targets. This may also make targets flinch.
